skill.name: obsidian-bases
skill.description: Create and edit Obsidian Bases (.base files) with views, filters, formulas, and summaries. Use when working with .base files, creating database-like views of notes, or when the user mentions Bases, table views, card views, filters, or formulas in Obsidian.
skill.summary: Create database-like views in Obsidian using .base files with filters, formulas, and multiple view types.
skill.author: kepano

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings are false positives from markdown code examples in documentation. The backtick patterns detected are markdown code formatting for Obsidian formula syntax examples, not actual shell execution. The cryptographic algorithm detections refer to date/duration formatting functions, not cryptographic operations. Hardcoded URLs are legitimate reference links to official Obsidian documentation. This is a documentation-only skill with no executable code.

content.user_title: Master Obsidian Bases for Database Views
content.value_statement: Transform your Obsidian vault into a powerful database with table, card, list, and map views. Create dynamic filters, formulas, and summaries without writing code.
actual_capabilities: Create .base files with YAML configuration for database-like views of notes
Build filters using tags, folders, properties, and dates with logical operators (and/or/not)
Write formulas for computed properties using arithmetic, conditional logic, and date functions
Configure multiple view types (table, cards, list, map) with custom column ordering
Add summary calculations like Average, Sum, Min, Max, Median, and Stddev
Embed bases in markdown notes and reference specific views
limitations: Only works within Obsidian with the Bases core plugin installed
Requires valid YAML syntax - small syntax errors will prevent the base from loading
Formulas must reference properties that exist on notes or use proper null checks
Date arithmetic returns Duration type, not numbers - requires accessing .days or .hours fields
Cannot execute arbitrary code or access external APIs - only works with local Obsidian data

use_cases.title: Task Management Dashboard
use_cases.description: Create a task tracker base that filters notes by task tags, shows priority levels with color-coded formulas, calculates days until due dates, and groups by status (Active, Completed, Backlog). Use summary formulas to see average completion time and overdue counts.

use_cases.title: Reading List Library
use_cases.description: Build a book and article tracking base with card views showing cover images, author names, and status icons. Use formulas to estimate reading time, filter by status (to-read, reading, done), and create grouped views by year or genre.

use_cases.title: Daily Notes Index
use_cases.description: Generate an index of daily notes with word count estimates, day of week calculations, and automatic filtering by date patterns. Create table views that show recent notes sorted by modification time with file metadata.
use_cases.target_user: Journal keepers and daily note practitioners

prompt_templates.title: Create Simple Task Base
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a .base file for a task tracker that filters notes tagged with 'task', shows a table view with file name, status, priority, and due date columns, and groups tasks by status.
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with basic task management in Obsidian

prompt_templates.title: Build Formula with Date Math
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a formula that calculates the number of days until a due date, returns empty string if no due date exists, and shows 'Overdue' if the date is in the past and status is not 'done'.
prompt_templates.scenario: Adding computed properties for deadline tracking

prompt_templates.title: Design Multi-View Base
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a .base file for a reading list with two views: a card view showing cover images and status icons for browsing, and a table view with author, pages, and estimated reading time columns for planning. Both views should exclude items with status 'dropped'.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating multiple perspectives on the same data

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Filter and Summary
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a base that filters notes from 'Daily Notes' folder matching date pattern YYYY-MM-DD, adds formulas for word count estimate and day of week, limits to 30 most recent notes, and shows average word count in summaries.
prompt_templates.scenario: Complex filtering with computed properties and aggregation

output_examples.input: Create a task tracker base with priority labels and overdue detection
output_examples.output: A complete .base file with filters for task tags, formulas showing emoji priority labels (High/Medium/Low), overdue detection using date comparison, table view grouped by status, and summary calculations for average days until due.

output_examples.input: Build a reading list with card and table views
output_examples.output: YAML configuration with formulas for reading time estimation and status icons, card view displaying cover images and titles, table view with author and page count, and filters to exclude dropped items.

best_practices: Always use single quotes around formulas containing double quotes to avoid YAML syntax errors
Include null checks with if() when referencing properties that may not exist on all notes
Access numeric fields like .days or .hours on Duration objects before applying math functions like .round()
Define formulas in the formulas section before referencing them as formula.X in properties or view order
anti_patterns: Using double quotes for formulas containing double quotes - this causes YAML parsing errors
Calling .round() directly on Duration objects without accessing .days or .hours first
Referencing formula.X in views without defining X in the formulas section
Using unquoted strings with special YAML characters like colons in display names

faq.question: Why does my base show a YAML error when I open it in Obsidian?
faq.answer: Check for unquoted strings containing special characters like colons or braces. Ensure formulas with double quotes are wrapped in single quotes. Verify all quotes are properly matched and nested.

faq.question: How do I calculate days between two dates?
faq.answer: Subtract the dates and access the .days field: '(date(due_date) - today()).days'. Duration objects require accessing a numeric field before using math functions.

faq.question: What is the difference between filters and view filters?
faq.answer: Global filters apply to all views in the base. View filters only apply to that specific view. Use global filters for the base scope and view filters for view-specific requirements.

faq.question: Can I use properties that do not exist on all notes?
faq.answer: Yes, but use null checks with if() to prevent errors: 'if(due_date, (date(due_date) - today()).days, "")'. This returns empty string if the property is missing.

faq.question: How do I show different summaries in my table view?
faq.answer: Add a summaries section to your view configuration mapping property names to summary types like Average, Sum, Min, Max, Median, or Stddev. These appear as footer rows in the table.

faq.question: What view types are available in Obsidian Bases?
faq.answer: Table view for columns and rows, cards view for visual galleries, list view for simple lists, and map view for geographic data requiring the Maps community plugin with latitude and longitude properties.
